cards.title: Having children makes you smarter
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than their non-parent peers. Years of mental and emotional demands of parenting appear to have measurable cognitive benefits.

cards.title: Family anxiety on holiday: learning to cope
cards.body: A parent's worry about worst-case scenarios can overshadow family time, but real emergencies revealed unexpected resilience. Discovering you can handle crisis situations builds confidence for future challenges.

cards.title: Adult attachment shaped by childhood relationships
cards.body: Dysfunctional parenting patterns—narcissistic and avoidant—can follow children into adulthood and affect their own relationships. Learning what healthy partnership looks like requires time and intentional work to break cycles.

cards.title: When adult children distance from parents
cards.body: Parents of adult children sometimes struggle with reduced contact despite maintaining close bonds. Understanding your grown child's boundaries helps preserve the relationship long-term.

cards.title: The power of developing executive function
cards.body: Executive function skills transform children's ability to manage time, attention, and emotions. Building these capabilities early creates lasting advantages across all life areas.

cards.title: Social media scrolling problems in youth
cards.body: Growing evidence documents serious health risks from problematic social media use among young people. Recent legal verdicts are setting important precedents for protecting youth wellness.
